guest.banner: Your project is saved locally. Sign in to save permanently.

guestEstimate.hintWorkType: Try naming the task after the work type, e.g. "Paint living room" or "Tile bathroom"
guestEstimate.hintDimensions: Add width and depth to the linked room to unlock estimates
guestEstimate.disclaimer: Based on average market rates. Actual costs depend on your contractor.
guestEstimate.laborOnlyDisclaimer: Labor only. Material costs depend on your choice of materials.
guestEstimate.signUpHint: Create a free account to save estimates, compare quotes, and track your renovation.
guestEstimate.tipProductivity: How many m² a professional typically completes per hour for this type of work
guestEstimate.tipMarketLow: Lower end of what contractors typically charge per hour
guestEstimate.tipMarketHigh: Upper end of what contractors typically charge per hour
guestEstimate.tipCoverage: Coverage: how many m² one liter of paint covers in one coat
guestEstimate.tipCoats: Number of coats of paint — typically 2 for good coverage
guestEstimate.tipWaste: Waste factor: extra material for cuts and waste — 1.1 means 10% extra
guestEstimate.tipUnitPrice: Average price per unit — varies by brand and quality

guestEstimate.activateHintTask: Add at least 1 task to activate
guestEstimate.activateHintRoom: Add at least 1 room to activate

taskTable.title: Your scope of work
taskTable.description: Start by listing everything that needs to be done — big or small. Painting, tiling, demolition, electrical... just type and add.

addTask.title: Add tasks here
addTask.description: Click this button to add a new task. You can edit names inline by clicking on them.

roomTable.title: Rooms & spaces
roomTable.description: Add all the rooms involved in your renovation. Each room can be linked to tasks for better organization.

addRoom.title: Add rooms
addRoom.description: Add rooms like Kitchen, Bathroom, Living room. You can enter dimensions to get automatic area calculations.

columns.title: Customize columns
columns.description: Show or hide columns to see room assignments, descriptions, and status for each task.

signUp.title: Save your work
signUp.description: Everything you add is saved locally. Create a free account to save permanently, get cost estimates, and invite collaborators.

landing.heroDescription: Renomate helps contractors, project managers and homeowners quote faster, stay on top of every project and collaborate seamlessly — from first client contact to final invoice.
landing.ho1Desc: Rooms, tasks, photos and notes – all in one place your contractors can access.
landing.ho2Desc: Invite painters, carpenters and electricians. Everyone sees only what's relevant to them.
landing.ho3Desc: Monitor costs, approve purchases and see exactly where your money goes.
landing.co1Desc: Collect client requirements via forms and generate quote materials instantly.
landing.co2Desc: Timeline, tasks and materials – all synced so you never lose track.
landing.co3Desc: Assign tasks to subcontractors and track their progress in real-time.
landing.ctaDescription: Create a free account and start organizing your project today
landing.featuresDesc: From floor planning to timeline tracking, everything you need in one place
landing.floorPlannerDesc: Draw rooms and plan your renovation visually

auth.signInDescription: Sign in to your Renomate account
auth.accountCreatedDescription: You can now sign in with your credentials.
auth.confirmEmailDescription: We sent a confirmation link to {{email}}. Click it to activate your account.

helpBot.title: Renomate Junior
helpBot.placeholder: Ask about renovations or how to use the app...
helpBot.disclaimer: NOTE: For building regulations I provide general guidance — always contact your municipality's building committee for final decisions.
helpBot.greeting:
Hi! I'm Renomate Junior — your renovation expert and platform guide. Ask me about building regulations, renovation tips, or how to get the most out of the app!

NOTE: For building regulations I provide general guidance — always contact your municipality's building committee for final decisions.

roleIntro.homeowner: I'm your personal renovation sidekick — here to help with everything from planning to punch list.
roleIntro.contractor: I'm your project assistant — ready to help with quotes, scheduling, and keeping things on track.
roleIntro.default: I'm your renovation expert and platform guide.

nudge.projects: Pick a project to dive into, or ask me anything about renovations!
nudge.project: I can see you're working on a project — ask me about next steps, building regulations, or how to use any feature.

helpBot.disclaimerShort: PS: For building regs I give general guidance — always double-check with your municipality.
helpBot.reminderIntro: I noticed a few things in your project:
helpBot.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again.
helpBot.bugPrompt:
Oh no, something's not working right? 🐛

Tell me what happened — for example:
• What did you click or do?
• What did you expect to happen?
• What happened instead?

The more details, the faster we can fix it! I'll automatically include which page you're on.
helpBot.suggestionPrompt:
Love that you want to help us improve! 💡

Tell me freely — is there a feature you're missing? Something that could work better? A workflow that feels clunky?

All ideas are welcome, big or small. We read every single one.
helpBot.otherPrompt: Of course! ✉️ Write whatever's on your mind — feedback, questions, or just a thought. I'll make sure the team sees it.
helpBot.bugSentConfirm:
Got it! 🙏 Your bug report has been sent straight to the development team. We take these seriously and will look into it.

Is there anything else you noticed?
helpBot.suggestionSentConfirm:
Thanks for the great input! 💡 Your suggestion has been forwarded to the team. We love hearing how we can make Renomate better.

Got more ideas?
helpBot.otherSentConfirm:
Message received! ✉️ The team will read it. Thanks for taking the time to write.

Anything else on your mind?
helpBot.feedbackError: Hmm, something went wrong sending your report. You can also email us directly at hello@letsrenomate.com — we read every message!
helpBot.feedbackCancelled: No worries, report cancelled. I'm still here if you need help with anything else!
helpBot.feedbackTypePicker: What would you like to share? Pick one below:
helpBot.addMorePrompt: Sure! Write away — I'll add it to your previous report.
helpBot.backToChatMsg: Back to normal! Ask me anything about renovations or the app. 😊
helpBot.bugModeLabel: Bug report — goes straight to the team
helpBot.suggestionModeLabel: Suggestion — goes straight to the team
helpBot.otherModeLabel: Message — goes straight to the team
helpBot.bugPlaceholder: What happened? What did you expect?

tips.title: Renovation Tips & FAQ
tips.subtitle: Answers to common questions about Swedish renovation permits, processes, and regulations
tips.disclaimer: This information is for general guidance only. Rules vary by municipality and may change. Always contact your local building committee (byggnadsnämnden) or relevant authority for binding decisions regarding your specific project.

whenBuildingPermit.title: When do I need a building permit (bygglov)?
whenBuildingPermit.content:
A building permit is required for most external changes to a building: new construction, extensions, changes to facades, new windows or doors that alter the appearance, changing use (e.g. garage to living space), and building new outbuildings over a certain size.

For single-family homes (one- and two-dwelling buildings), some changes are permit-free, such as painting or re-roofing in the same style, building a small attefall house (max 30 m²), or adding a dormer.

Always check with your municipality — rules can vary depending on your local plan (detaljplan).

permitProcess.title: What does the building permit process look like?
permitProcess.content:
1. Prepare drawings (site plan, floor plans, facades, sections)
2. Submit application to your municipality's building committee
3. The committee reviews the application (normally within 10 weeks)
4. Neighbors may be consulted
5. If approved, you receive a building permit decision
6. Before construction starts, you must have a start notification (startbesked)
7. After completion, you need a final clearance (slutbesked) before use

permitCost.title: How much does a building permit cost?
permitCost.content:
Costs vary by municipality and project scope. A typical building permit for a single-family home extension might cost SEK 10,000–40,000. More complex projects or commercial buildings cost more.

The fee covers the municipality's review and administration. There is no national fixed price — contact your municipality for their fee schedule (taxa).

whenNotification.title: When is a building notification (bygganmälan) required?
whenNotification.content:
A building notification is required for work that doesn't need a building permit but still affects the building's construction, such as:

• Installing or significantly changing load-bearing structures
• Installing or changing ventilation systems
• Installing or changing plumbing and sewage
• Installing a fireplace or stove
• Changing the floor plan inside a building
• Demolition work

You must receive a start notification (startbesked) before work begins.

notificationVsPermit.title: Difference between building permit and building notification
notificationVsPermit.content:
Building permit (bygglov): Required for external changes, new buildings, and changes in use. Involves a more thorough review including neighborhood impact.

Building notification (anmälan): Required for internal technical changes that affect the building's structural integrity, safety, or technical systems. The process is simpler and faster.

Some projects require both a permit and a notification.

technicalConsultation.title: What is a technical consultation (tekniskt samråd)?
technicalConsultation.content:
For larger or more complex projects, the building committee may require a technical consultation before issuing a start notification.

At this meeting you discuss construction plans, quality documentation, inspections, and safety. A certified control manager (kontrollansvarig, KA) is usually required for these projects.

rotDeduction.title: What is the ROT deduction?
rotDeduction.content:
ROT (Renovation, Conversion, Extension) is a tax deduction for labor costs on renovation work in your home. You get 30% off the labor cost (not materials), up to SEK 50,000 per person per year.

The deduction is applied directly on the invoice — you only pay 70% of the labor cost. The contractor claims the rest from Skatteverket.

rotRules.title: Who is eligible for ROT deduction?
rotRules.content:
You must:
• Be at least 18 years old
• Own (or co-own) the property
• Live or plan to live in the property
• Have sufficient tax to deduct against
• Use an F-tax registered contractor

ROT applies to houses, apartments (bostadsrätt), and holiday homes you own. It does not apply to rental apartments or new construction.

rotEligible.title: What renovation work qualifies for ROT?
rotEligible.content:
Eligible work includes:
• Painting and wallpapering
• Floor laying
• Bathroom renovation
• Kitchen renovation (labor only)
• Electrical work
• Plumbing work
• Masonry and tiling
• Roof repairs
• Drainage and ground work

Not eligible: new construction, purchases of materials/appliances, cleaning, garden work (that's RUT).

contractTypes.title: What contract should I use with my contractor?
contractTypes.content:
For consumer contracts with contractors, the standard templates are recommended:

• Hantverkarformuläret 17 — For smaller renovation jobs
• ABS 18 — For building a new house with a contractor
• Småhus 22 — For turnkey house construction

These contracts are developed jointly by consumer and industry organizations and provide balanced terms for both parties. Never start work without a written contract.

quotesOffers.title: Fixed price vs. hourly rate — what should I choose?
quotesOffers.content:
Fixed price (fast pris): The contractor commits to a total price. You know the cost upfront. Best for well-defined projects where the scope is clear.

Hourly rate (löpande räkning): You pay for actual time spent. Can be cheaper for small or uncertain jobs, but the final cost is unknown. The contractor must work efficiently and the price must be reasonable.

Tip: Always get at least 2-3 quotes. If a quote seems very low, ask what's included — it may exclude important items.

consumerRights.title: What are my rights if something goes wrong?
consumerRights.content:
Under the Consumer Services Act (konsumenttjänstlagen):

• The work must be done professionally (fackmässigt)
• The contractor must warn you about risks or unexpected issues
• You can claim defects for up to 10 years after completion
• If the work is defective, you can demand correction at no cost
• You can withhold payment proportional to the defect
• In serious cases, you can cancel the contract

Always document defects with photos and notify the contractor in writing.

contractorInsurance.title: Should my contractor have insurance?
contractorInsurance.content:
Yes, always verify that your contractor has:

• Liability insurance (ansvarsförsäkring) — covers damage to your property during work
• F-tax registration — means they handle their own taxes
• Check for any tax debts or issues at Skatteverket

You can verify F-tax status at skatteverket.se. Ask the contractor to show their insurance certificate before work starts.

warrantyRules.title: How long is the warranty on renovation work?
warrantyRules.content:
Under Swedish consumer law, you can claim defects for up to 10 years after the work was completed. However, you should report defects as soon as you discover them (within 'reasonable time').

Many contractors offer a specific warranty period (1-5 years) — this does not limit your legal rights but may make the claims process easier during that period.

Always do a thorough inspection (slutbesiktning) when the work is finished.

homeInsurance.title: Does my home insurance cover renovation damage?
homeInsurance.content:
Home insurance typically covers sudden and unforeseen damage, but usually NOT:
• Damage caused by the contractor's negligence (that's their liability insurance)
• Gradual damage from poor workmanship
• Cost overruns or delays

Check with your insurance company before major renovations. Some insurers want to be notified about large renovation projects. Extended coverage may be available.

condoPermission.title: What renovations can I do in my condo (bostadsrätt)?
condoPermission.content:
As a condo owner, you are responsible for the interior of your apartment. You can generally:
• Paint and wallpaper
• Change flooring
• Replace kitchen cabinets and appliances
• Change interior doors

You need board permission for:
• Changes to load-bearing walls
• Changes to plumbing, drainage, or ventilation
• Changes that affect the building's exterior
• Any work that may affect other apartments

Check your association's statutes (stadgar) for specific rules.

condoWetRooms.title: Renovating bathrooms in a condo — special rules
condoWetRooms.content:
Bathroom renovations in condos require extra care:

• You need board approval in most associations
• Waterproofing (tätskikt) must be done by a certified professional (e.g. GVK or BKR certified)
• The work must follow BBR (Boverkets byggregler) standards
• Your association may require specific contractors or materials
• You are liable for water damage to other apartments caused by faulty renovation

Always use certified professionals and get proper documentation.

condoLiability.title: Who is liable for damage during condo renovation?
condoLiability.content:
You are personally responsible for all work done inside your apartment, even if a contractor performs the work. This means:

• You must ensure the contractor is qualified and insured
• You are liable for damage to common areas or other apartments
• Water damage from a faulty bathroom renovation is your responsibility
• The association can require you to restore things if you've made unauthorized changes

Tip: Get a separate renovation insurance (renoveringsförsäkring) for major projects.

demoProject.title: Apartment Renovation - Example
demoProject.description: Explore this example project to learn how Renomate works

rooms.livingRoom: Living room with hardwood floors and crown molding. Recently plastered and painted.
rooms.bedroom: Bedroom with space for double bed. To be wallpapered with patterned wallpaper.
rooms.kitchen: Kitchen with dining area. New countertop and backsplash planned.
rooms.bathroom: Bathroom with shower and washing machine. Complete tiling planned.
rooms.hallway: Hallway with closet and coat rack. Original floor sanding.

preparation.title: Protect and prepare
preparation.description: Cover floors and furniture with protective paper and plastic. Tape edges at ceiling and windows.

spackling.title: Wall spackling
spackling.description: Repair cracks and holes in walls. Sand and prime before painting.

paintingLivingRoom.title: Living room painting
paintingLivingRoom.description: Paint walls with wall paint (2 coats) and ceiling with ceiling paint. Color: NCS S 0502-Y.

wallpaper.title: Bedroom wallpapering
wallpaper.description: Apply patterned wallpaper on feature wall. Other walls painted in matching color.

floorSanding.title: Floor sanding
floorSanding.description: Sand hardwood floors in living room and hallway. Apply 3 coats of floor lacquer.

kitchenSurfaces.title: Kitchen - countertop & backsplash
kitchenSurfaces.description: Install new laminate countertop and tile backsplash behind stove.

bathroomTiling.title: Bathroom tiling
bathroomTiling.description: Install white 20x20 tiles on walls and floor tiles. Including waterproofing membrane.

trim.title: Install trim
trim.description: Install new baseboards in all rooms and crown molding in living room.

electrical.title: Kitchen lighting
electrical.description: Install LED spotlights in ceiling and under-cabinet lighting.

finalCleaning.title: Final cleaning
finalCleaning.description: Construction cleaning of entire apartment. Remove protective paper and vacuum thoroughly.

roleModal.title: How would you like to explore?
roleModal.subtitle: Choose your perspective to see the demo

homeownerContext.overview: You are viewing your own project — this is how it looks when you manage a renovation yourself.
homeownerContext.customerView: You are viewing the Client View — this is what you see when a builder invites you to follow your project.

overview.title: Your Project Dashboard
overview.body:
This is the home page for you as a homeowner when you manage your own renovation project — with help from builders, painters, and other professionals.

Here you get full insight into tasks, material purchases, budget, and more.
overview.tip: Tip: The summary cards at the top give you a quick snapshot of the project's status.

customer.title: Client View
customer.body:
This page is available when you as a client have been invited by your building contractor to follow the renovation process.

Here you can see the timeline, keep track of finances, and communicate with your project team.
customer.tip: Tip: This is a simplified view — your builder controls what information is shared here.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.body:
Here you can see all the tasks in your renovation — from demolition and electrical work to painting and final cleaning.

Each task shows status, schedule, and who is responsible.
tasks.tip: Tip: Click on a task to see full details and any linked materials.

purchases.title: Purchases
purchases.body:
Here you'll find all material orders and purchases for your project — tiles, fixtures, countertops, and more.

You can track status from order to delivery.
purchases.tip: Tip: Drag cards between columns to update the status of a purchase.

budget.title: Budget
budget.body:
The budget page gives you full control over your renovation's finances.

See what has been quoted, what has been paid, and what remains for each item.
budget.tip: Tip: Use the Columns button to add extra columns like 'Paid' and 'Outstanding'.

spaceplanner.title: Space Planner
spaceplanner.body:
The floor plan gives you a visual overview of your home's rooms and layout.

View room details, checklists, and linked tasks for each space.
spaceplanner.tip: Tip: Click on a room to see all tasks and materials connected to it.

files.title: Files
files.body:
All project documents are collected here — quotes, invoices, drawings, photos, and more.

Everything organized and accessible in one place.

overview.title: Welcome — Planning Phase
overview.body:
You're starting in the Planning phase — where every project begins. Here you define the scope, add tasks, and prepare a quote for the client.

When you're ready, use the phase stepper in the bar above to switch to 'Quote sent' and 'Active project' to see how the project evolves through each stage.
overview.tip: Tip: Try all three phases! Each one unlocks different tools and views tailored to that stage of the project.

tasks.title: Task Management
tasks.body:
Plan and track all tasks in the project. Assign team members, set schedules, link materials, and monitor progress.

Switch between table and kanban view depending on your workflow.
tasks.tip: Tip: Drag tasks between columns in kanban view to quickly update their status.

purchases.title: Purchase Orders
purchases.body:
Manage material orders, track deliveries, and keep spending under control.

Each order can be linked to a task and room for full traceability.
purchases.tip: Tip: Use the filter button to quickly find orders by status, room, or vendor.

budget.title: Budget & Margins
budget.body:
Full financial overview with customer prices, actual costs, margins, and material budgets.

Inline-edit values directly in the table for fast updates.
budget.tip: Tip: The 'Result' column shows your margin per task — red means you're over budget.

spaceplanner.title: Space Planner
spaceplanner.body:
Draw floor plans, define rooms, and link tasks and materials to specific spaces.

Use the room checklist to track renovation scope per room.
spaceplanner.tip: Tip: Switch to 'Floor Plan' in the sub-tabs to use the full drawing canvas.

files.title: Project Files
files.body:
Collect all project documents — quotes, invoices, drawings, contracts, and photos.

Link files to specific tasks or materials for easy reference.

team.title: Team Management
team.body:
Invite subcontractors, clients, and team members to your project.

Control exactly what each person can see and do with granular permissions.
team.tip: Tip: Use the role presets to quickly set appropriate access levels for clients vs. team members.

externalQuotes.title: Imported quotes
externalQuotes.subtitle: Quotes received from builders outside the app

inviteCustomerPlanning.title: Invite customer to plan
inviteCustomerPlanning.description: The customer gets access to add tasks and rooms — nothing else. No pricing, no other tabs.
inviteCustomerPlanning.noPricing: No pricing, budget, or cost information
inviteCustomerPlanning.sentDescription: The customer will receive an email with a link to fill in their renovation scope.

shareRfq.title: Share quote request
shareRfq.description: Share your renovation plan with builders to receive quotes.
shareRfq.inviteDescription: The builder gets access to your scope and rooms, and can create a quote for you.
shareRfq.sentDescription: The builder will receive an email with a link to view your renovation plan and create a quote.

rfqBanner.title: Quote request from homeowner
rfqBanner.description: Review the scope below, add your pricing, then create a quote.

bearingWall.title: Load-bearing wall — permit required
bearingWall.body: Removing or modifying a load-bearing wall requires a structural engineer's assessment and a building permit. Always consult your municipality before starting.

buildingPermit.title: Building permit may be required
buildingPermit.body: Extensions, facade changes, balconies, and covered patios typically require a building permit. Check with your municipality — processing times can be 6–10 weeks.

wetRoom.title: Wet room — use certified contractor
wetRoom.body: Bathroom waterproofing (tätskikt) must be done by a GVK or BKR-certified contractor for the warranty to be valid. Always ask for certification before hiring.

electrical.title: Electrical work — licensed electrician required
electrical.body: In Sweden, all electrical installations must be performed by a licensed electrician. DIY electrical work is illegal and voids insurance.

plumbing.title: Plumbing — use certified installer
plumbing.body: Plumbing work (water and sewer) should be done by a Säker Vatten-certified installer to ensure warranty coverage and insurance validity.

rot.title: ROT deduction — save up to 50,000 SEK/year
rot.body: As a homeowner, you can deduct 30% of labor costs for renovation work (ROT). Max 50,000 SEK per person per year. The contractor applies the deduction on the invoice.

rotBuilder.title: Remember ROT deduction on quotes
rotBuilder.body: Many homeowners expect ROT deduction to be shown on quotes and invoices. Showing the price both before and after ROT makes your quote more competitive.

planningChecklist.title: Planning your renovation
planningChecklist.body: Start by listing all rooms and tasks. Get at least 3 quotes, check contractor references, and agree on a written contract before work begins.

planningBuilder.title: Planning phase — scope the project
planningBuilder.body: Help the homeowner define clear deliverables for each task. Detailed scope descriptions reduce change orders and disputes later.

compareQuotes.title: Compare quotes carefully
compareQuotes.body: Look beyond the total price. Compare what's included (materials, waste removal, permits), payment terms, timeline, and warranty conditions.

quoteSpecificity.title: Be specific in your quotes
quoteSpecificity.body: Detailed quotes with line items for labor, materials, and timeline build trust and reduce back-and-forth. Include what's excluded to avoid scope creep.

condo.title: Condo — check with your association
condo.body: Renovations in condos (bostadsrätt) often require approval from the housing association (BRF), especially for bathrooms, plumbing, and structural changes.

paintingPrep.title: Painting — preparation is key
paintingPrep.body: Good painting results depend on surface preparation. Budget time for filling holes, sanding, priming, and taping. Typically 60–70% of the work is prep.

firstProject.title: Welcome to your first project!
firstProject.body: Start by adding rooms and tasks to define your renovation scope. You can then share it with contractors to get quotes, or track progress as work begins.

insurance.title: Check your insurance coverage
insurance.body: Before renovation starts, verify that your home insurance covers construction damage and that your contractor has liability insurance. Ask for proof.

addTasks.title: Start by adding your renovation tasks
addTasks.body: List everything that needs to be done — painting, tiling, plumbing, electrical. This forms the foundation of your project plan.

setDeadline.title: Set a project deadline
setDeadline.body: A target completion date helps you plan the work sequence and track whether the project is on schedule.

inviteBuilder.title: Ready to invite a builder?
inviteBuilder.body: You have tasks listed — consider sending a quote request (RFQ) to builders to get price estimates for the work.

setBudget.title: Set your project budget
setBudget.body: Track your spending against a budget to avoid surprises. You can adjust it anytime as quotes come in.

reviewBudget.title: Time to review your budget
reviewBudget.body: Your project is over halfway done. Check how actual spending compares to your budget and make adjustments if needed.

builderAddTasks.title: Define the scope of work
builderAddTasks.body: Add the tasks you'll be performing on this project. This helps create accurate quotes and track progress.

inviteCustomer.title: Invite the customer to collaborate
inviteCustomer.body: Share the project with your customer so they can review scope, provide input, and stay updated on progress.

updateProgress.title: Update task progress
updateProgress.body: Keep your project status current by marking completed tasks. This keeps all stakeholders informed.

sendUpdate.title: Send a status update
sendUpdate.body: Your customer will appreciate a quick update on how the project is progressing. Photos are always welcome!

noTasks.title: No tasks created yet
noTasks.body: Start by listing the work that needs to be done.

noRooms.title: No rooms added
noRooms.body: Add rooms to connect tasks to specific spaces.

unlinkedTasks.title: Tasks not linked to rooms
unlinkedTasks.body: Connect tasks to rooms for better organization and estimates.

noDeadline.title: No project deadline set
noDeadline.body: Set a target completion date to track your schedule.

noStartDate.title: No start date set
noStartDate.body: Set when the project begins for timeline tracking.

rotPersonnummer.title: ROT: Personal ID number missing
rotPersonnummer.body: Required for ROT tax deduction claims.

rotProperty.title: ROT: Property designation missing
rotProperty.body: Add your property designation (fastighetsbeteckning) for ROT eligibility.

noBudget.title: No budget set
noBudget.body: Set a budget to track spending and avoid surprises.

budgetOver.title: Budget exceeded
budgetOver.body: Your spending has exceeded the project budget.

analysis.title: ROT deduction
analysis.checkRotDetails: ROT details filled in (personal ID, property)
analysis.noDataForYear: No costs recorded for this year

smartUpload.title: Smart Upload
smartUpload.dropHere: Drag & drop or click to select
smartUpload.supportedTypes: Quote, invoice, receipt, floor plan, contract, images...
smartUpload.classifyError: Could not analyze the document. Please try again.
smartUpload.linkPrompt: Is this an existing item or do you want to create a new one?

linkPurchase.description: Link to an existing task or create a new purchase.
linkPurchase.canvasAdded: The drawing has been added to the canvas.

quoteReview.title: Review quote
quoteReview.description: Review extracted tasks and prices before importing.
quoteReview.importResult: {{tasks}} tasks and {{rooms}} rooms added to the project

dm.emptyHint: Messages here are only visible to you two.
